title: Electromagnetic Radiation in Communication — PYP Mastery

questions.question: Which row in the table correctly identifies a property of digital and analogue signals?nn| | Analogue Signal | Digital Signal |n| :--- | :--- | :--- |n| A | Continuous values | Fixed values only |n| B | Fixed values only | Continuous values |n| C | High frequency only | Low frequency only |n| D | No noise | Includes noise |
questions.explanation:
Directly from the Syllabus Map and Map It sections: Analogue is continuous, while digital is fixed. 

**Common mistake:** Swapping the two properties.

questions.question: Microwaves are used for satellite communication. Why are they chosen for this application?
options: They can pass through the Earth's atmosphere without being reflected
They travel faster than radio waves in a vacuum
They are completely unaffected by noise and interference
They have very long wavelengths that can curve around the Earth
questions.explanation:
Microwaves can penetrate the atmosphere to reach satellites, whereas some radio frequencies are reflected. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking microwaves travel faster than other EM waves.

questions.question: A digital signal is transmitted over a long distance. During transmission, the signal becomes weak and picks up noise. What happens at a regenerator station?nn[Diagram: Shows a messy pulse becoming a clean square pulse]
options: The circuit produces a clean version of the original signal
The noise is amplified to make the signal louder
The signal is slowed down to improve accuracy
The signal is turned into a continuous analogue wave
questions.explanation:
A regenerator station identifies the digital 1s and 0s and produces a new, noise-free signal. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking the station just 'cleans' the existing signal rather than creating a new one.

questions.question: Which types of electromagnetic radiation are used in optical fibre communication?
questions.explanation:
Visible light and infrared are the standard regions used for optical fibres. 

**Common mistake:** Selecting Row C, as microwaves are common for other communications.

questions.question: A geostationary satellite orbits at a height where the time for one orbit is 24 hours. What is the average orbital speed if the orbit radius is $42000\text{ km}$?
questions.explanation:
$v = (2\pi r) / t = (2 \times 3.14 \times 42000000) / (24 \times 3600) \approx 3054\text{ m/s} \approx 3100\text{ m/s}$. 

**Common mistake:** Using the altitude instead of the orbital radius, or failing to convert time to seconds.
